system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{

    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,nuTilda) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

vertices

            (1 2)
        );
    }
    frontAndBack
    {

        faces
        (
            (0 3)
            (1 2)
            (4 7)
            (5 6)
        );
    }
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='channel.stl', folder_name='constant/triSurface', content='// L-shaped channel walls
// Bottom wall (y=0): from x=-20.6 to x=206
facet normal 0 1 0
{
    outer loop
    {
        vertex -20.6 0 0
        vertex 0 0 0
        vertex 206 0 0
    }
}

// Top wall (y=25.4): from x=-20.6 to x=206
facet normal 0 -1 0
{
    outer loop
    {
        vertex -20.6 25.4 0
        vertex 206 25.4 0
        vertex 0 25.4 0
    }
}

// Left wall (x=-20.6): from y=0 to y=25.4
facet normal -1 0 0
{
    outer loop
    {
        vertex -20.6 0 0
        vertex -20.6 25.4 0
        vertex -20.6 25.4 0.5
    }
}

// Right wall (x=206): from y=0 to y=25.4
facet normal 1 0 0
{
    outer loop
    {
        vertex 206 0 0
        vertex 206 25.4 0
        vertex 206 0 0.5
    }
}

// Back wall (z=0.5): connect all walls at z=0.5
facet normal 0 0 -1
{
    outer loop
    {
        vertex -20.6 0 0.5
        vertex 0 0 0.5
        vertex 206 0 0.5
    }
}

facet normal 0 0 -1
{
    outer loop
    {
        vertex 0 25.4 0.5
        vertex 206 25.4 0.5
        vertex -20.6 25.4 0.5
    }
}

// Front wall (z=0): connect all walls at z=0
facet normal 0 0 1
{
    outer loop
    {
        vertex -20.6 0 0
        vertex 0 0 0
        vertex 206 0 0
    }
}

facet normal 0 0 1
{
    outer loop
    {
        vertex 0 25.4 0
        vertex 206 25.4 0
        vertex -20.6 25.4 0
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='snappyHexMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{

}

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

geometry
{
    channel.stl
}

castellatedMesh true;

snap            true;

addLayers       false;

layers
{
}

refinementSurfaces
{
    "inlet|outlet"
    {
        level (2);
    }
    "upperWall|lowerWall"
    {
        level (2);
    }
}

maxLocalCells   500000;
maxCells        1000000;
minCells        1000;
cellSizeCrit    1;
maxCo           0.4;
maxDeltaP       0.1;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.3;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeInterval   0.01;

purgeWrite      0;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

dynamicFvMesh   yes;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.5;')]</foamfiles>
<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.pimpleFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Cannot find file "points" in directory "polyMesh" in times "0" down to constant

    From function virtual Foam::IOobject Foam::fileOperation::findInstance(const Foam::IOobject&, Foam::scalar, const Foam::word&) const
    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block index out of range for patch face 2(2 6)
    Number of blocks = 1, index = 2
    on patch outlet, face 0

file: outlet from line 41 to line 45.

    From function void Foam::blockMesh::checkPatchLabels(const Source&, const Foam::word&, const Foam::pointField&, Foam::faceList&) const [with Source = Foam::dictionary; Foam::pointField = Foam::Field<Foam::Vector<double> >; Foam::faceList = Foam::List<Foam::face>]
    in file blockMesh/blockMeshTopology.C at line 57.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

Both errors persist from all previous attempts:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: `Block index out of range for patch face 2(2 6)` — the outlet patch references face index 2 but only has 2 faces (indices 0 and 1).
2. **`log.pimpleFoam`**: Cannot find "points" — cascading failure from blockMesh failing.

## Root Cause Analysis

The error `"Block index out of range for patch face 2(2 6)"` is clear and definitive. The `blockMesh` tool is reading the `blockMeshDict` and finding that the **outlet patch specifies face `(2 6)`**, but when the single hex block is analyzed, face index 2 refers to a different face than `(2 6)`. The face indexing in the block's internal face ordering doesn't match what the boundary patch definitions expect.

Looking at the current `blockMeshDict` with a single block `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)`:

The six internal faces are ordered as:
- Face 0: `(0 1 2 3)` — z = -0.6 (bottom)
- Face 1: `(4 5 6 7)` — z = +0.6 (top)
- Face 2: `(1 2 5 6)` — y = min (this is the OUTLET face!)
- Face 3: `(0 3 4 7)` — y = max
- Face 4: `(0 4)` — x = min (INLET)
- Face 5: `(1 5)` — x = max

The problem is that the outlet patch wants face `(2 6)` — but face 2 is `(1 2 5 6)` (a quadrilateral) and face 1 is `(4 5 6 7)`. Neither equals `(2 6)` alone. The outlet face `(2 6)` consists of **two separate edge vertices**, meaning it's expecting the outlet to be a **single face made of vertices 2 and 6 connected by an edge** — but in a hex block, no single face is just two vertices.

**The fundamental fix:** The outlet patch should reference the **correct internal face index** that corresponds to the outlet. That is face 2 `(1 2 5 6)`. So change the outlet definition from:

```
outlet
{
    type wall;
    faces
    (
        (2 6)
    );
}
```

to:

```
outlet
{
    type wall;
    faces
    (
        (2 6)
    );
}
```

Wait — that's the same. The issue is that `(2 6)` alone doesn't form a complete face. **The outlet face must be specified as the full face index.** In the single-hex-block topology, the outlet face is **face 2** which is `(1 2 5 6)`. The boundary patch should reference it as:

```
outlet
{
    type wall;
    faces
    (
        (2)
    );
}
```

Similarly, the inlet face is face 4 `(0 4)`, so:

```
inlet
{
    type patch;
    faces
    (
        (4)
    );
}
```

And `upperWall` (face 3 = `(0 3 4 7)`) becomes:

```
upperWall
{
    type wall;
    faces
    (
        (3)
    );
}
```

And `lowerWall` consists of faces 0 and 1, so:

```
lowerWall
{
    type wall;
    faces
    (
        (0)
        (1)
    );
}
```

And `frontAndBack` faces 0 and 1 become:

```
frontAndBack
{
    type empty;
    faces
    (
        (0)
        (1)
    );
}
```

## Summary

1. **`system/blockMeshDict`**: Change all boundary patch `faces` entries from edge-pair notation like `(0 4)` to **single-face index notation** like `(4)`. Each face in the boundary patch should reference the **internal face index** of the hex block, not vertex edge descriptions. Specifically:
   - `inlet`: `(4)` instead of `(0 4)`
   - `outlet`: `(2)` instead of `(2 6)`
   - `upperWall`: `(3)` instead of `(3 7)`
   - `lowerWall`: `(0)` and `(1)` instead of `(0 1)` and `(1 2)`
   - `frontAndBack`: `(0)` and `(1)` instead of `(0 3)`, `(1 2)`, `(4 7)`, `(5 6)`

2. **After fixing blockMeshDict**, re-run `blockMesh` followed by `pimpleFoam`.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Change inlet faces from (0 4) to (4); Change outlet faces from (2 6) to (2); Change upperWall faces from (3 7) to (3); Change lowerWall faces from (0 1) and (1 2) to (0) and (1); Change frontAndBack faces from (0 3), (1 2), (4 7), (5 6) to (0) and (1)'}, {'file': 'system/controlDict', 'changes': 'Change maxCo from 0.5 to 0.4'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a Reynolds-Averaged Simulation (RAS) pitzdaily simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm. The domain is a 2D millimeter-scale channel geometry. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 15m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls. Use timestep of 0.0001 and output every 0.01. Finaltime is 0.3. use nu value of 1e-04.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
